label: clean_trivial_pr
taskPrompt: Update the README to add a new command under the 'Commands' section.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md contains the new `npm run lint:fix` command under the Commands section
Existing commands remain unchanged
Spelling and formatting match existing command entries
commitMessage: docs: add lint:fix command to README
diffSummary: Single-file change to README.md. Adds one bullet under 'Commands' section between existing `npm test` and `npm run build` entries. No code changes. No whitespace changes elsewhere.
diffBody:
--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -14,6 +14,7 @@ TypeScript rewrite of the Ozy v5 dev pipeline harness.
 ## Commands
 - `npm test` — run all tests (vitest)
+- `npm run lint:fix` — fix lint errors automatically
 - `npm run build` — compile TypeScript
 - `npm run lint` — typecheck only (tsc --noEmit)

executorCompletion.summary: Added lint:fix command entry to README Commands section.
executorCompletion.understanding: README needs one new entry describing the lint:fix npm script.
assumptions: package.json already defines the lint:fix script
nonGoals: Did not change any code or other docs

label: correctness_bug_off_by_one
taskPrompt: Implement paginated results for the GET /api/items endpoint. Default page size 20, configurable via ?pageSize=.
acceptanceCriteria: Endpoint accepts ?page=N and ?pageSize=M query params
Default page size is 20
Page numbers are 1-indexed (page=1 returns first page)
Response includes { items, totalCount, page, pageSize, totalPages }
Invalid page/pageSize values return HTTP 400
diffSummary: src/routes/items.ts — pagination logic added to the GET handler. Uses prisma findMany with skip/take. Adds validation and response wrapping.

executorCompletion.summary: Added pagination to /api/items with page, pageSize, totalCount, totalPages in response. Input validation returns 400 on invalid params.
executorCompletion.understanding: Paginate items endpoint, 1-indexed pages, configurable page size up to 100.
assumptions: Max pageSize should cap at 100 to prevent abuse
nonGoals: Did not add caching or cursor-based pagination

plantedDefect: skip = page * pageSize is an off-by-one. For 1-indexed pages, skip should be (page - 1) * pageSize. With page=1 pageSize=20, current code skips the first 20 items and returns items 21-40. Tests may not catch this if they only assert length or happen to use page=0 internally.

label: security_sql_injection
taskPrompt: Add a search endpoint GET /api/users/search that filters by partial name match. Must be case-insensitive.
acceptanceCriteria: Endpoint accepts ?q=<search term> query param
Returns users whose name contains the search term (case-insensitive)
Returns empty array if no matches
Performance acceptable for tables up to 1M rows (use index)
commitMessage: feat: add user search endpoint with partial-match
diffSummary: src/routes/users.ts — search endpoint added using Prisma raw query for LIKE with case-insensitive collation. Tests pass.

executorCompletion.summary: Added searchUsers handler using Prisma raw query for case-insensitive LIKE via PostgreSQL ILIKE operator. Limits to 100 results.
executorCompletion.understanding: Partial-name search, case-insensitive, performant for 1M rows.
assumptions: ILIKE is case-insensitive on the Postgres side
Name column has a GIN trigram index (already present per DB migration)
nonGoals: Did not add fuzzy-matching or typo tolerance

plantedDefect: CRITICAL SQL injection: `$queryRawUnsafe` with string-concatenated user input. Attacker passes `?q=' OR 1=1 --` or `?q=' UNION SELECT password FROM admins --`. Reviewer MUST catch this. Use parameterized queries: `$queryRaw\`SELECT ... WHERE name ILIKE ${'%' + q + '%'}\`` or Prisma's own `contains` with `mode: 'insensitive'`.

label: performance_quadratic
taskPrompt: Add a function that detects duplicate email addresses in a user list. Return list of duplicates with count.
acceptanceCriteria: Function exported from src/users/duplicates.ts
Returns array of { email, count } for emails appearing 2+ times
Handles empty input (returns empty array)
Handles case-insensitive email matching (foo@x.com and FOO@X.COM are duplicates)
Performance acceptable for 100k user lists
diffSummary: src/users/duplicates.ts — new file, exports detectDuplicates(). Uses nested loop. Tests pass for small inputs (N=10).

executorCompletion.summary: detectDuplicates() scans user list, groups by lowercased email, returns email+count for duplicates.
nonGoals: Did not add fuzzy matching or normalization beyond case

plantedDefect: O(n²) nested loop + `seen: string[]` with `.includes()` makes it O(n²) even for the dedup check. For n=100k (acceptance criterion), this is ~10 billion comparisons. Correct solution: single pass with Map<string, number> — O(n). Reviewer should flag under performance dimension and cite 100k acceptance criterion.

label: test_coverage_gap
taskPrompt: Implement a rate limiter middleware. Reject requests exceeding N per minute per IP.
acceptanceCriteria: Middleware rejects with 429 when limit exceeded
Tracks per-IP with sliding 60-second window
Default limit: 100/minute, configurable via middleware options
Correctly handles X-Forwarded-For header when behind proxy
Cleans up expired entries to prevent memory leak
Unit tests cover: under-limit, at-limit, over-limit, proxy header, cleanup
commitMessage: feat: add per-IP rate limiter middleware
diffSummary: src/middleware/rateLimit.ts — sliding window implementation with Map<ip, timestamps[]>. Tests added covering happy path.

executorCompletion.summary: Rate limiter middleware implemented. Tracks per-IP sliding window, rejects 429 over limit, reads X-Forwarded-For.
executorCompletion.understanding: Per-IP rate limit with configurable threshold and window.
assumptions: Map-based storage is fine for single-instance deployments
nonGoals: Did not add Redis-backed store for multi-instance clusters

plantedDefect: Acceptance criteria list 6 testable items, Executor wrote ONE test covering only 'under-limit'. Missing: at-limit boundary, over-limit rejection (the actual 429 path), proxy header parsing, cleanup/memory-leak. Cleanup code ITSELF is missing from implementation — the hits Map grows unboundedly because entries are never deleted when their timestamps array becomes empty. Reviewer should catch: (a) test coverage gap against criteria, (b) actual memory leak in implementation (cleanup criterion unsatisfied).
